page: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7R13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. The instance achieves top-10% results in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Passmark CPU scores, and Redis SET operations, which reach 129.17 million operations per second. Memory performance is strong, with small-block cached read bandwidth in the top 10% and large-block uncached read/write speeds in the top 25%. Single-core performance, measured via Geekbench and stress-ng, falls into the strong to average categories, while LLM inference prompt processing for small and medium models shows strong throughput.

Given its high resource density and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the m6a.32xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale enterprise workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The lack of local NVMe storage means storage performance depends on network-attached volumes, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores. It features 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations, alongside strong results in database operations and static web serving. While single-core speeds are moderate, its massive parallel processing capacity makes it highly efficient for multi-threaded enterprise workloads. It is well-suited for database hosting, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual server delivering 128 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.

512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
